nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Tene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Breton (Pène, rarely Le Pène): variant of Pen and, in North America, possibly also an altered form of this.
Slovenian: diminutive or patronymic of   Pen   and, in North America, possibly also an altered form of this.

variants.name: Pen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Cambodian Chhem, Chhum, Roeuth, Phal, Saroeun, Sokha, Boeun, Chan, Chhay, Chheng, Choung, Han, Heng, Hoeung, Kong, Lei, Leang, Leng, Nip, Nong, Noy, Phoeun, Samnang, Samoeun, Savath, Somaly, Sophath, Sopheap, Sheng, Soeun, Sophal, Tha, Yen.  Russian Iosif, Lev, Oleg.
Dutch: metonymic occupational name for a clerk or penman, from pen ‘pen’, or for a craftsman using pins, from pen ‘pin’ (compare Pin ).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: Alice de Tene was born in 1150, in Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of William Tene -Lord of Tene. She had at least 1 son with William I Geun of Ridware. She died in 1215, at the age of 65.
